heading: Trial before judges
label: 2505 Trial before judges
text: Any judge of the United States Court of Federal Claims may sit at any place within the United States to take evidence and enter judgment.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., §§ 275 and 275a (, ; , ; , ; , ).Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 16736 Stat. 1140Feb. 24, 1925, ch. 301, § 243 Stat. 965June 23, 1930, ch. 573, § 146 Stat. 799Oct. 16, 1941, ch. 44355 Stat. 741Changes were made in phraseology.

Amendments1992— substituted “United States Court of Federal Claims” for “United States Claims Court”.Pub. L. 102–5721982— substituted “United States Claims Court” for “Court of Claims” and “enter judgment” for “report findings”.Pub. L. 97–1641954—Act , substituted “Trial before judges” for “Place of taking evidence” in section catchline and repealed second par. relating to taking of testimony.Sept. 3, 1954
Effective Date of 1992 AmendmentAmendment by effective , see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 102–572section 911 of Pub. L. 102–572section 171 of this titleOct. 29, 1992
